title: Area between two curves given end points
description: Calculate the area that's bounded by functions from above and below. In this exercise, the right and left endpoints of the area are given.
topic_path: Finding the area between curves expressed as functions of x
Area between two curves given end points

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the area of the region between the graphs of $f(x)=\dfrac{2x}{3\pi}-\dfrac{5}{3}$ and $g(x)=\cos(x)$ from $x=2\pi$ to $x=\dfrac{5}{2}\pi$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Visualizing the area

We sketch the graphs of $~f~$ and $~g~$ first.

![graph](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ba74854e07999df587a7d8d17949fbf1735f55b1)

From the graph, it appears that $g(x)\ge f(x)$ between $x=2\pi$ and $x=\dfrac52\pi$ . From this we are looking to evaluate:

$\displaystyle \int_{2\pi}^{5\pi/2}\left( g(x)-f(x) \right)\,dx$

assessment_items.question_content:
The curves $f(x)=\sin x$ and $g(x)=\cos x$ intersect periodically. 

**Determine the area of the region bounded by these curves between $x=\dfrac{\pi}{4}$ and $x=\dfrac{5\pi}{4}$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Visualizing the area

We sketch the curves $f(x)=\sin x$ and $g(x)=\cos x$ first.

![graph](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/26f2409a9e295233afa932f7b184fec4d4f71c4e)

From the graph, it appears that $f(x) \ge g(x)$ between $x=\dfrac{\pi}4$and $x=\dfrac{5\pi}4$.

From this we are looking to evaluate:

$\displaystyle \int_{{\pi}/{4}}^{{5\pi}/{4}}\left( f(x)-g(x) \right)\,dx$

##Evaluating the definite integral

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\int_{\pi/4}^{5\pi/4}\left(\sin x-\cos x\right)\,dx
\\\\
&=\left(-\cos x-\sin x\right)~\Bigg|_{\pi/4}^{5\pi/4} \\\\
&=-\left(\cos x+\sin x\right)~\Bigg|_{\pi/4}^{5\pi/4} \\\\
&=-\left(-\dfrac{\sqrt2}{2}-\dfrac{\sqrt2}{2}\right)+\left(\dfrac{\sqrt2}{2}+\dfrac{\sqrt2}{2}\right) \\\\
&= 2\sqrt2
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the area of the region between the graphs of $f(x)=3e^x$ and $g(x)=-2$ from $x=0$ to $x=3$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Visualizing the area

We sketch the graphs of $f$ and $g$ first.

![graph](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1a5439d36a46204e9deb3e4f39801dff7b230671)

From the graph, it appears that $f(x)\ge g(x)$ between $x=0$ and $x=3$ . From this we are looking to evaluate:

$\displaystyle \int_{0}^{3}\left( f(x)-g(x) \right)\,dx$
